action.reasoning: Observation: Best confirmed test balanced_accuracy is 0.5606 from iteration 13 (domain-specific ensemble). Iteration 17's re-evaluation of the same checkpoint scored only 0.5533, likely due to a predict.py bug (ResNet features not flattened). Iteration 19's prototype eval scored 0.4264. We have ~40 min and 11 iterations left. Analysis: The highest priority is to fix predict.py so ResNet outputs are properly flattened to 2D, and features are L2-normalized per backbone before concatenation — matching how the classifiers were trained. Then re-evaluate the best known checkpoint (domain_ensemble.pkl) to confirm the pipeline is correct. As a bonus, I'll create a domain-tuned variant by tuning per-class intercepts on validation (cheap). Decision: Write a robust, offline-safe predict.py with correct flattening and normalization. Write make_domain_tuned.py to tune domain ensemble intercepts. Run the tuning script, then submit evaluation for domain_ensemble.pkl immediately.
